title: Stylesheet organization

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling draft for Stylesheet organization; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CSS Cascading and Inheritance Level 5; W3C specification accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Stylesheet organization
sources.claim: A maintainable CSS system separates tokens, base rules, layout primitives, components, utilities, and controlled overrides with documented cascade layers.

sources.locator: MDN CSS Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Stylesheet organization
sources.claim: A maintainable CSS system separates tokens, base rules, layout primitives, components, utilities, and controlled overrides with documented cascade layers.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A maintainable CSS system separates tokens, base rules, layout primitives, components, utilities, and controlled overrides with documented cascade layers.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: The project orders reset, base, components, and utilities in layers so overrides do not depend on import accidents. Your goal is to refactor a conflicting stylesheet into a predictable layered structure. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Specificity and source order; html semantics; Custom properties and design tokens; Pseudo-classes and pseudo-elements. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: A maintainable CSS system separates tokens, base rules, layout primitives, components, utilities, and controlled overrides with documented cascade layers. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Naming alone cannot prevent leakage, excessive utility combinations obscure intent, and global resets can affect third-party content. This draft therefore rejects: Adding !important wherever a conflict appears is a scalable organization strategy.

includes: Separate reset/base, layout, component and utility concerns with documented layering and naming.
Refactor a conflicting stylesheet into a predictable layered structure.
excludes: Naming alone cannot prevent leakage, excessive utility combinations obscure intent, and global resets can affect third-party content.

outcomes: Refactor a conflicting stylesheet into a predictable layered structure.

examples.title: Stylesheet organization: concrete case
examples.situation: The project orders reset, base, components, and utilities in layers so overrides do not depend on import accidents.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: The project orders reset, base, components, and utilities in layers so overrides do not depend on import accidents.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Define ownership and cascade layers, keep specificity low, and reserve important rules for explicit policy rather than conflict repair.
examples.result: The result follows because Define ownership and cascade layers, keep specificity low, and reserve important rules for explicit policy rather than conflict repair.

examples.title: Stylesheet organization: boundary case
examples.situation: Naming alone cannot prevent leakage, excessive utility combinations obscure intent, and global resets can affect third-party content.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Naming alone cannot prevent leakage, excessive utility combinations obscure intent, and global resets can affect third-party content.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: Adding !important wherever a conflict appears is a scalable organization strategy.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Define ownership and cascade layers, keep specificity low, and reserve important rules for explicit policy rather than conflict repair.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Adding !important wherever a conflict appears is a scalable organization strategy.
misconceptions.correction: Define ownership and cascade layers, keep specificity low, and reserve important rules for explicit policy rather than conflict repair.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling artifact to explain why this belief fails: Adding !important wherever a conflict appears is a scalable organization strategy.

analogy.example: A layered stage design can model Stylesheet organization, where rules size, place, and paint existing content.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Stylesheet organization before using exact platform terms
limitations: Physical layers do not reproduce cascade algorithms, intrinsic sizing, writing modes, or user preferences.
